codeReview.summary: The feature adds the requested native download/read surface and covers the happy-path/save-dir contract, but the PDF validation is too weak: a record-dependent provider can return a mislabeled non-PDF response and the CLI will still persist it and report success.

issues.description: `looksLikePDF` accepts any response whose `Content-Type` contains `application/pdf` even when the body is not actually a PDF. `retrievePaperPDF` relies on that check before writing the file, so a provider that serves an HTML/login/error page with a misleading PDF content type will be recorded as a successful native download/read and leave a stray non-PDF file behind. That breaks the feature contract that record-dependent retrieval should succeed only when a real public file exists.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `go-connector-worker` skill lacks a documented verification-only path for reassigned features that are already implemented, forcing the worker to deviate from the tests-first procedure even though the deviation was justified.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/go-connector-worker/SKILL.md steps 4-6 require writing failing tests before implementation, while the handoff for session ee32b62a-47c4-4dac-a143-697c2666f627 records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false` because the feature was already present in commit f5f5dde and the worker only re-validated it.

summary: Reviewed the transcript skeleton, handoff, architecture/skill context, and commit f5f5dde. The feature is close, but it has a blocking correctness gap in native PDF detection that can falsely treat non-PDF responses as successful record-dependent retrievals.
